items.headline: Get Your Latest NBA News From RealGM's Basketball Wiretap
items.blurb: Get Your Latest NBA News From RealGM's Basketball Wiretap — Get all the latest news and happenings from around the NBA with RealGM's Basketball Wiretap.

items.headline: Nuggets' Nikola Jokic joins elite company with performance vs. Spurs
items.blurb: Nuggets' Nikola Jokic joins elite company with performance vs. Spurs — Before Victor Wembanyama took the NBA by storm, the league was marveling over the dominance of Nikola Jokic. And on Saturday night, the veteran legend made history while getting the better of the younger star.

items.headline: UConn Advances To Third National Title Game In Four Years
items.blurb: UConn Advances To Third National Title Game In Four Years — UConn defeated Illinois 71-62 in the Final Four on Saturday night to make their third national title game in four years. Tarris Reed Jr. lead the Huskies with 17 points. Braylon Mullins added 15 points, going 4-7 from three. UConn lead by as much as 14 points in the second half before Illinois cut the lead to four points with less than fi

items.headline: Lauren Betts, UCLA Defense Beat Texas In Low-Scoring Final Four Semifinal
items.blurb: Lauren Betts, UCLA Defense Beat Texas In Low-Scoring Final Four Semifinal — UCLA ended Texas's tournament run Friday in a grinding 51-44 semifinal victory, securing the program's first-ever NCAA championship game berth. The Bruins will face South Carolina on Sunday. Lauren Betts delivered 16 points and 11 rebounds to pace UCLA. The win avenged the Bruins' only defeat of the season, a 76-65 loss to

items.headline: Joel Embiid Sits Sixers' Loss To Pistons
items.blurb: Joel Embiid Sits Sixers' Loss To Pistons — Joel Embiid missed the Philadelphia 76ers' 116-93 loss to the Detroit Pistons on Saturday due to "right oblique; injury management; [and] illness." Embiid expressed his frustration over getting ruled out of Wednesday's game against the Wizards on Friday. Embiid has not played in both back-to-back games this season. Nick Nurse said he wasn't concerned with

items.headline: Nikola Vucevic Questionable To Return Sunday Versus Raptors
items.blurb: Nikola Vucevic Questionable To Return Sunday Versus Raptors — The Boston Celtics upgraded Nikola Vucevic to questionable for Sunday's game against the Toronto Raptors. Vucevic has been out for a month after undergoing surgery for a fractured right ring finger. Vucevic is averaging 10.4 points and 7.2 rebounds in 12 games with the Celtics.

items.headline: Steph Curry Says Managing Knee Injury Is 'New Normal'
items.blurb: Steph Curry Says Managing Knee Injury Is 'New Normal' — Golden State Warriors guard Steph Curry discussed his frustration in dealing with an "unpredictable" knee injury ahead of his expected return on Sunday against the Houston Rockets. Curry has missed 27 games with "runner's knee." "I thought I was going to be out a week," Curry said. "Ten days max. [But] every time I got on the court or tried t

items.headline: Pistons beat 76ers, clinch Eastern Conference top seed
items.blurb: Pistons beat 76ers, clinch Eastern Conference top seed — Tobias Harris scored 19 points, and Daniss Jenkins added 16 points and 14 assists as the Detroit Pistons beat the Philadelphia 76ers 116-93 Saturday night to clinch the top seed in the Eastern Conference playoffs.

items.headline: How the Lakers can use major injuries to their advantage
items.blurb: How the Lakers can use major injuries to their advantage — The injuries to Reaves and Dončić may close the door on this season, but they could quietly open a much bigger one for the future in Los Angeles.

items.headline: Celtics Sign Ron Harper Jr. To Two-Year Contract
items.blurb: Celtics Sign Ron Harper Jr. To Two-Year Contract — The Boston Celtics are signing Ron Harper Jr. to a two-year contract, his agent told Shams Charania of ESPN. Harper was previously on a two-way contract. Harper is averaging 3.4 points and 1.6 rebounds on the season.

items.headline: Nikola Jokic continues late MVP push with epic performance in OT win over Spurs
items.blurb: Nikola Jokic continues late MVP push with epic performance in OT win over Spurs — Nikola Jokic continued his late push for a fourth MVP trophy with an unreal performance in a 136-134 overtime win against the San Antonio Spurs and fellow MVP candidate Victor Wembanyama.

items.headline: Hawks Sign Tony Bradley For Rest Of Season; Waive Caleb Houstan
items.blurb: Hawks Sign Tony Bradley For Rest Of Season; Waive Caleb Houstan — The Atlanta Hawks are signing Tony Bradley for the rest of the season, sources told Shams Charania of ESPN. The Hawks are waiving Caleb Houstan to make room for Bradley. Bradley provides front court depth with Jock Landale set to miss at least two weeks with a high ankle sprain.

items.headline: 2025-26 NBA MVP Prediction: Why Victor Wembanyama Has the Strongest Case
items.blurb: 2025-26 NBA MVP Prediction: Why Victor Wembanyama Has the Strongest Case — With the 2025-26 NBA regular season entering its final stretch, the MVP race has come down to three truly elite candidates: Victor Wembanyama, Shai Gilgeous-Alexander, and Nikola Jokic. All three have delivered seasons worthy of serious consideration, and in many years, each one would have a legitimate argument to win the a

items.headline: Jokic scores 40, outduels Wembanyama as Nuggets top Spurs in OT
items.blurb: Jokic scores 40, outduels Wembanyama as Nuggets top Spurs in OT

items.headline: Lakers' Austin Reaves out for remainder of season
items.blurb: Lakers' Austin Reaves out for remainder of season — The Los Angeles Lakers announced that Austin Reaves will miss the remainder of the regular season after being diagnosed with a Grade 2 left oblique muscle injury.

items.headline: Giannis Antetokounmpo, Bucks split feeling more inevitable after latest feud
items.blurb: Giannis Antetokounmpo, Bucks split feeling more inevitable after latest feud — The latest details come a day after the league opened an investigation into whether the Bucks, who have been eliminated from playoff contention, have prevented Antetokounmpo from playing.

items.headline: Austin Reaves Expected To Miss 4-6 Weeks With Oblique Injury
items.blurb: Austin Reaves Expected To Miss 4-6 Weeks With Oblique Injury — Austin Reaves was diagnosed with a Grade 2 left oblique muscle injury and will be out for the rest of the regular season, the Los Angeles Lakers announced on Saturday. While the Lakers didn't give a timetable for Reaves' return, he is expected to miss the next 4-6 weeks. Reaves, who underwent an MRI on Saturday, suffered the injury on

items.headline: Nuggets’ Jokic cool under pressure with two clutch shots to seal win
items.blurb: Nuggets’ Jokic cool under pressure with two clutch shots to seal win — The Denver Nuggets and San Antonio Spurs trade baskets late in overtime, with Nikola Jokic, who finished with 40 points, 13 assists, and eight rebounds, hitting two crucial buckets in a big sequence to help clinch the Nuggets’ 136-134 victory.

items.headline: Lakers’ Austin Reaves out four to six weeks with oblique injury
items.blurb: Lakers’ Austin Reaves out four to six weeks with oblique injury — Guard Austin Reaves is set to miss four to six weeks after suffering a Grade 2 oblique injury, ESPN’s Shams Charania reported Saturday.

items.headline: LeBron James disses two more American cities after profane rant about Memphis
items.blurb: LeBron James disses two more American cities after profane rant about Memphis — LeBron James is fully embracing his “get off my lawn” era.

items.blurb: Basketball Intelligence For 4/4/26 — The day's best basketball news and analysis.

items.headline: Candace Parker, Amar'e Stoudemire, Doc Rivers Headline 2026 Hall Of Fame Class
items.blurb: Candace Parker, Amar'e Stoudemire, Doc Rivers Headline 2026 Hall Of Fame Class — Candace Parker, Amar'e Stoudemire, and Doc Rivers were announced as part of a nine-member class set to be inducted into the Basketball Hall of Fame this upcoming August. They will be joined by Elena Delle Donne, Mark Few, Joey Crawford, Chamique Holdsclaw, Mike D'Antoni and the 1996 United States Women's National Team

items.headline: Bucks Tell NBA Giannis Antetokounmpo Declined Participating In 3-On-3 Scrimmages During Return Process
items.blurb: Bucks Tell NBA Giannis Antetokounmpo Declined Participating In 3-On-3 Scrimmages During Return Process — The Milwaukee Bucks told NBA investigators that Giannis Antetokounmpo declined participating in 3-on-3 scrimmages as part of his return protocol, sources told Shams Charania of ESPN. Antetokounmpo has told the league that he is healthy enough to return to action from a hyperextended left knee a

items.headline: Joel Embiid Expresses Frustration Missing Wednesday Game: 'I Wasn't Allowed To Play Basketball'
items.blurb: Joel Embiid Expresses Frustration Missing Wednesday Game: 'I Wasn't Allowed To Play Basketball' — Joel Embiid voiced his frustration over missing the Philadelphia 76ers' 153-131 win over the Washington Wizards on Wednesday. Embiid missed the game due to a reported illness. "I was pissed off," Embiid said Friday. "I wanted to play basketball. I wasn't allowed to play basketball. So, this is more of

items.headline: Knicks-Thunder Matchup Averages 3.4M Viewers On NBC
items.blurb: Knicks-Thunder Matchup Averages 3.4M Viewers On NBC — Last weekend's matchup between the New York Knicks and Oklahoma City Thunder for NBC's "Sunday Night Basketball" averaged 3.4 million viewers across Nielsen and Adobe Analytics. It was the largest NBA audience during March Madness since 2016. The Warriors-Nuggets nightcap averaged 3.0 million viewers. The NBA does not traditionally show games o

items.headline: Cooper Flagg On Scoring Career-High 51 Points: 'The Basket Feels Big'
items.blurb: Cooper Flagg On Scoring Career-High 51 Points: 'The Basket Feels Big' — Cooper Flagg scored a career-high 51 points in the Dallas Mavericks' 138-127 loss to the Orlando Magic on Friday night. Flagg, who shot 19-of-30 from the field, also became the youngest player in league history to score 50 points. "It's always fun getting into that type of mode," said Flagg after the game. "The basket feels bi

items.headline: Nets Sign Malachi Smith To Two-Year Deal
items.blurb: Nets Sign Malachi Smith To Two-Year Deal — The Brooklyn Nets are signing Malachi Smith to a two-year contract, sources told Shams Charania of ESPN. Smith averaged 7.3 points and 2.1 rebounds across two 10-day contracts. The Nets also announced on Friday that Michael Porter Jr. and Danny Wolf are out for the rest of the season.

items.headline: Angry Joel Embiid calls out Daryl Morey: 'I wasn't allowed to play'
items.blurb: Angry Joel Embiid calls out Daryl Morey: 'I wasn't allowed to play' — After sitting out Wednesday’s win in Washington due to an illness, Philadelphia 76ers center Joel Embiid was back in action on Friday, registering 19 points, 13 rebounds and seven assists across 34 minutes of action in a home victory over Minnesota.

items.headline: NBA Playoff Race 2026: Pistons, Thunder and March’s Standings Shake-Up
items.blurb: NBA Playoff Race 2026: Pistons, Thunder and March’s Standings Shake-Up — Source Pistons and Thunder are driving March, but the chase is getting louder March is the month when the NBA stops pretending there is time. The standings tighten, the excuses get shorter, and every hot week starts to look like a playoff identity test. That is why the NBA playoff race 2026 feels sharper now than it did even

items.headline: Ailing Anthony Edwards struggles in Timberwolves loss to 76ers
items.blurb: Ailing Anthony Edwards struggles in Timberwolves loss to 76ers — Edwards came back from a knee injury Monday and looked like he hadn't skipped a beat after missing two weeks with a knee injury. Then an illness derailed him as the Minnesota Timberwolves are scrambling for playoff seeding.

items.headline: Nikola Jokic: Pours in 40 against Wemby
items.blurb: Nikola Jokic: Pours in 40 against Wemby — Jokic notched 40 points (13-25 FG, 1-5 3Pt, 13-15 FT), eight rebounds, 13 assists and three blocks in 44 minutes during Saturday's 136-134 overtime win over San Antonio. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Victor Wembanyama: Loses OT duel with Joker
items.blurb: Victor Wembanyama: Loses OT duel with Joker — Wembanyama posted 34 points (8-17 FG, 2-6 3Pt, 16-17 FT), 18 rebounds, seven assists, five blocks and one steal over 40 minutes during Saturday's 136-134 overtime loss to the Nuggets. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Kel'el Ware: Huge performance off bench Saturday
items.blurb: Kel'el Ware: Huge performance off bench Saturday — Ware supplied 24 points (10-15 FG, 3-6 3Pt, 1-2 FT), 19 rebounds, two assists and seven blocks in 36 minutes off the bench during Saturday's 152-136 win over the Wizards. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Jaime Jaquez: Erupts for game-high 32 in win
items.blurb: Jaime Jaquez: Erupts for game-high 32 in win — Jaquez totaled 32 points (12-18 FG, 1-2 3Pt, 7-7 FT), three rebounds, four assists and one steal in 32 minutes off the bench during Saturday's 152-136 victory over the Wizards. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: GG Jackson: Doubtful for Sunday
items.blurb: GG Jackson: Doubtful for Sunday — Jackson is doubtful to play Sunday in Milwaukee due to left knee soreness. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Fire Select Bridget Carleton With No. 1 Pick In Expansion Draft
items.blurb: Fire Select Bridget Carleton With No. 1 Pick In Expansion Draft — The Portland Fire selected unrestricted free agent Bridget Carleton with the No. 1 pick in the 2026 WNBA expansion draft on Friday. Carleton averaged 6.5 points and 3.6 rebounds for the Lynx last season. The Tempo selected point guard Julie Allemand with their top pick in the draft. With Carleton and Tempo draft pick Marina Mabrey b

items.headline: Karl-Anthony Towns Sits Win Over Bulls With Elbow Injury
items.blurb: Karl-Anthony Towns Sits Win Over Bulls With Elbow Injury — Karl-Anthony Towns missed the New York Knicks' 136-96 win over the Chicago Bulls due to right elbow impingement. Towns participated in pre-game warmups before getting ruled out. Mitchell Robinson tooks Towns' place in the starting lineup. Towns is averaging 20.1 points and 11.9 rebounds on the season.

items.headline: Michael Porter Jr., Danny Wolf Out For Rest Of Season
items.blurb: Michael Porter Jr., Danny Wolf Out For Rest Of Season — The Brooklyn Nets are shutting down Michael Porter Jr. and Danny Wolf for the rest of the season, according to coach Jordi Fernandez. Wolf is out with a left ankle injury, with Porter dealing with a left hamstring strain. Porter averaged 24.2 points and 7.1 rebounds in 52 games this season. Porter will be eligible for an extension for up to f

items.headline: Grange: Memphis didn’t deserve LeBron James’ criticism
items.blurb: Grange: Memphis didn’t deserve LeBron James’ criticism — LeBron James’ harsh words about Memphis were a tough pill to swallow for Grizzlies fans. It may be tough for Memphis to thrive in today’s NBA, but no one deserves to hear criticism like that.

items.headline: Latest Luka Doncic injury update may have big implications for Lakers star
items.blurb: Latest Luka Doncic injury update may have big implications for Lakers star — The hamstring injury suffered by Los Angeles Lakers superstar Luka Doncic on Thursday night against the Oklahoma City Thunder is severe enough that it could keep him out for an extended period of time. The initial timetable is not exactly encouraging for the Lakers, or for his chances of winning major NBA awards and honor

items.headline: Cooper Flagg's historic scoring night can't get Mavericks a win
items.blurb: Cooper Flagg's historic scoring night can't get Mavericks a win — Cooper Flagg became the first teenager to score 50 points in an NBA game Friday. That wasn't enough to get the Dallas Mavericks a win.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Magic 138, Mavericks 127
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Magic 138, Mavericks 127 — Wendell Carter posted 28 points on 50 per cent shooting, while Desmond Bane added 27 points and three steals as the Orlando Magic earned a 138-127 win over the Dallas Mavericks. Cooper Flagg tallied a new career-high with 51 points in the loss.

items.headline: Mavericks’ Cooper Flagg becomes first teen to score 50 points
items.blurb: Mavericks’ Cooper Flagg becomes first teen to score 50 points

items.headline: Mavericks' Cooper Flagg was bound to make NBA history
items.blurb: Mavericks' Cooper Flagg was bound to make NBA history — Even during another loss for the Dallas Mavericks, rookie Cooper Flagg continued to make his debut season historic. The No. 1 overall pick from the 2025 NBA Draft put together a performance the league had never seen before.

items.headline: Mavericks’ Naji Marshall, coach Jason Kidd tossed vs. Magic
items.blurb: Mavericks’ Naji Marshall, coach Jason Kidd tossed vs. Magic

items.headline: How Raptors’ Barrett backed up talk with dominant performance vs. Grizzlies
items.blurb: How Raptors’ Barrett backed up talk with dominant performance vs. Grizzlies — Brad Fay, Sherman Hamilton, and Matt Bonner break down the Toronto Raptors’ blowout win against the Memphis Grizzlies and explain how forward RJ Barrett was able to back up his talk with a dominant performance on the court.

items.headline: Raptors coast past Grizzlies to snap two-game skid
items.blurb: Raptors coast past Grizzlies to snap two-game skid — RJ Barrett scored 25 points, Brandon Ingram had 17 points and seven rebounds and the Toronto Raptors built a first-half lead, extended it in the third and coasted to a 128-96 victory over the Memphis Grizzlies on Friday night.

items.headline: ‘We can beat anybody’: Raptors’ Barrett confident ahead of Celtics matchup
items.blurb: ‘We can beat anybody’: Raptors’ Barrett confident ahead of Celtics matchup — RJ Barrett spoke to the media following the Toronto Raptors decisive win over the Memphis Grizzlies, saying they kept their foot on the gas from tip-off to final buzzer, adding that he’s eager to face the Boston Celtics in the team’s next matchup.

items.headline: NBA Investigating Bucks Over Giannis Antetokounmpo Availability Dispute
items.blurb: NBA Investigating Bucks Over Giannis Antetokounmpo Availability Dispute — The NBA is investigating the Milwaukee Bucks over their handling of the player participation policy and potentially inconsistent statements regarding Giannis Antetokounmpo's health, sources told ESPN's Shams Charania on Friday.
